REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS GLORIA YAM AND CHARLES KIM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 19, 2019. MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT. OPPOSING PARTY IS ORDERED COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF OPPOSITION. COURTESY COPIES TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 501 NO LATER THAN APRIL 4, 2019 WITH COVER LETTERS REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED (D501)
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-19-2019 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GLORIA YAM, CHARLES KIM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FIRST, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO BOTH DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED ANY ALLEGATIONS THAT CHARLES KIM WAS PARTY TO ANY ORAL AGREEMENT, MADE ANY PROMISE TO DOMINIC. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE ENOUGH FACTS THAT EITHER DEFENDANT OBTAINED A SPECIFIC BENEFIT THROUGH FRAUD OR FOR WRONGFUL USE. DEMURRER IS ALSO SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS DEMONSTRATING THE APPLICABILITY OF PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF MAY, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, ALLEGE ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON NEW OR DEVELOPING FACTS. PLAINTIFF REQUESTS 30 DAYS AMEND. DEFENDANT DOES NOT OBJECT. THE COURT GRANTS THE REQUEST. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of James A. Lassart
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adrian G. Driscoll, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, T:415.788.1900, for defendants/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS GLORIA YAM and CHARLES KIM's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs is Granted. No opposition filed.

In addition, the Court grants defendant's counsel's request to add the fees and costs to the language of the order and also to attach a copy of the Lis Pendens as exhibit.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Off Calendar Jul-19-2019 Continued To Sep-25-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Continued To Jul-31-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Anthony Burchell, 415.757.4505 of Legal Assistance to the Elderly for Plaintiff Dominic Yam.
Kristin Iversen, 415.788.1900 of Murphy, Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendant Gloria Yam.

Argued by Plaintiff. Issues argued: fraud is an essential element of a quashi contract. The issue of unjust enrichment for the Defendant.

The Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT GLORIA YAM, CHARLES KIM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend First, Third, and Fourth causes of action as to both Defendants. Plaintiff has not pled any allegations that Charles Kim was party to any oral agreement, made any promise to Dominic. Plaintiff fails to allege enough facts that either Defendant obtained a specific benefit through fraud or for wrongful use. Demurrer is also SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to Second cause of action. Plaintiff has not alleged facts demonstrating the applicability of promissory estoppel. Plaintiff may, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, allege additional causes of action based on new or developing facts. Plaintiff requests 30 days amend. Defendant does not object. The Court grants the request. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
